DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MAY-03-2021 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF WILLIS LEASE FINANCE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH AFRIJET BUSINESS SERVICES RESPONSES TO WLFCS FIRST INSPECTION DEMAND, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR FURTHER RESPONSES, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MOTION GRANTED - NO OPPOSITION FILED. DEFENDANT AFRIJET BUSINESS SERVICE MUST SERVE CODE-COMPLIANT FURTHER RESPONSES AND/OR PRODUCE ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS ON OR BEFORE MAY 15, 2021. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT WILLIAMCLYNN@GMAIL.COM. calendar.matters:
COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Apr-25-2022 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Short Cause Trial/Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant and cross-complainant Afrijet Business Service, a Gabonese company.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 11:49 a.m., adjourning at 11:55 a.m. and reconvening at 11:59 a.m.

Thomas V. Loran, Esq. appeared for plaintiff and cross-defendant Willis Lease Finance Corporation.
Dean Poulakidas, General Counsel, Senior Vice President and Custodian of Records for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
Messrs. Loran and Poulakidas appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendant and cross-complainant Afrijet Business Service, a Gabonese company.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Aircraft Lease and Engine Lease Agreements filed November 19, 2019.
Defendant Afrijet Business Services' Answer to plaintiff's Complaint, and Cross-Complaint, were filed on March 18, 2020.
Plainitff's Request for Judicial Notice was filed with the Clerk's Office on November 8, 2021.
DOE defendants dismissed March 24, 2022.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff & Cross-Defendant Willis Lease Finance Corporation, and against the Defendant and Cross-Complainant Afrijet Business Service, a Gabonese Company, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $3,923,322.81.
Prejudgment Interest - $1,003,322.81.
Attorney Fees - TBD.
Costs - $2,251.33.

Total - $4,929,514.83.

Counsel for plaintiff and cross-defendant to submit an updated attorney fee declaration along with a proposed Judgment for the Court's review and signature.

Proceedings concluded at 12:44 p.m.

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-28-2022 Continued To Apr-25-22 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 514
